chunks.page_summary: This page describes the 'Dataview' feature within the Rheonics Control Panel software, explaining how to open it, its interface elements, and how it displays and logs measured parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Dataview

Modified on: Thu, 28 Apr, 2022 at 4:55 PM

Rheonics Dataview Windows offers an extended and customized view for measured parameters.

1.Opening dataview

When the dataview button is clicked from the measurements tab, a new window is opened.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.3.1.6

Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Communication, Logger, Help

Measured Values:
  Viscosity: 2952.20 mPa.s
  Density: 1.0000 g/cc
  Temperature: 32.33 °C
  Pressure: 1.00 bar

Status:
  Running... E00
  Logging: Stopped
  Pressure: Manual
  Manual entry: 1 bar

Log Measurements:
  Sample Name (input field)
  Start Logging (button)
  Add Event (button)

Sensor Information:
  Serial Number (input field)
  Fres: 7428.36 Hz
  df: 0.96 Hz

Graph 1 (Top):
  Y-axis Left: Viscosity [mPa.s]
  Y-axis Right: Density [g/cc]
  X-axis: hh:mm:ss (00:00:00 to 00:05:43)

Graph 2 (Bottom):
  Y-axis Left: Temperature [°C]
  Y-axis Right: Pressure [bar]
  X-axis: hh:mm:ss (00:00:00 to 00:05:43)

Figure 1. Opening dataview

2. Measurements View

The measurements window by default shows two graphs, as shown in picture below; the first one plots density and viscosity, the second plots temperature and pressure.
1. On the top of the window, current measurement values are shown.
2. By clicking the settings button, the “Settings” page is viewed.
3. On the left side of the first plot a tools palette allows to move, vertical zoom, horizontal zoom, and fit the plot. When one of the tools is selected the control is highlighted and the mouse pointer changes when passed over the plot area. The tool can be disabled by clicking it again.
4. Shows the plot area of the first plot.
key_facts: Rheonics Dataview Windows provides an extended and customized view for measured parameters.
The dataview window opens when the dataview button is clicked from the measurements tab.
The measurements window displays two graphs by default.
The first graph plots density and viscosity.
The second graph plots temperature and pressure.
Current measurement values are displayed at the top of the window.
Clicking the settings button navigates to the 'Settings' page.
A tools palette on the left side of the first plot enables functions like moving, vertical zooming, horizontal zooming, and fitting the plot.
When a tool from the palette is selected, the control is highlighted and the mouse pointer changes over the plot area.
A selected tool can be disabled by clicking it again.

key_facts: The document describes the 'Tools palette' and 'Plot area' of a display.
Figure 2 illustrates a 'Measurement view' within the Rheonics Dataview software.
The software displays real-time measurements for Viscosity, Density, M.W (Molecular Weight), Temperature, and Pressure.
Clicking the 'Graph Fitting' button will display a fit graph on the screen.
The tools palette includes options for hand (panning), zoom in, zoom out, autoscale, and crosshairs.

3. Settings View
3.1. When the “Settings” button is clicked the windows switches to a settings page (Refer to image below).
key_facts: To take a snapshot, users must click the 'Camera' button, which will open a save screen to store the image.
Clicking the 'Settings' button will navigate the application window to a dedicated settings page.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays the Rheonics DataView interface with real-time process parameters (viscosity, density, molecular weight, temperature, and pressure) and provides instructions for window navigation and plot customization within the application.

Figure 5.Settings window

3.2. Window navigation
1. Click this button to go back to “measurements” page.
2. Y axis settings for plot 1; the parameters to be plotted on each one of the two axes can be selected. Also, the graph itself can be made visible or not visible by checking the “Display plot” control.
3. Y axis settings for plot 2.
4. X axis settings; in this section the plot time can be modified. Top is 24 hours for real time plot update. It is independent of the time of the RCP graphs.
5. For Molecular Weight Only: The DataView window has the capability of calculating the molecular weight for ideal gases. If Molecular weight is plotted on any of the charts, the y axis range, units and resolution can be modified. The calculation of molecular weight can be disabled.
key_facts: The Rheonics DataView application displays real-time data for viscosity, density, molecular weight, temperature, and pressure.
Users can configure Y-axis settings for two separate plots, allowing selection of parameters to be plotted on either of two axes.
Individual plots can be made visible or invisible using a 'Display plot' control.
The X-axis plot time can be modified, with real-time plot updates available for up to 24 hours.
The Rheonics DataView can calculate molecular weight specifically for ideal gases.
If molecular weight is plotted, its y-axis range, units, and resolution are modifiable, and its calculation can be disabled.
The described navigation feature allows returning to a 'measurements' page, implying the current view or context may be a settings-related page.
The plot time update is independent of the time of the RCP graphs.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays the settings interface of Rheonics Dataview software, allowing users to configure Y-axis plots, X-axis time intervals, and molecular weight display options, while also showing current sensor measurements.
chunks.extracted_text:

Figure 6. Settings View

For viscosity, density, temperature and pressure units and ranges, and for time interval, the Dataview takes the settings from the “Settings” tab of RCP.
key_facts: The Rheonics Dataview software displays real-time measurements for viscosity, density, molecular weight (M.W.), temperature, and pressure.
The Y-axis settings allow configuration of two separate plots, each with primary and secondary axes.
Plot 1 is configured to display Density on the Primary Axis and Viscosity on the Secondary Axis.
Plot 2 is configured to display Temperature on the Primary Axis and Pressure on the Secondary Axis.
The X-axis plot time is currently set to 10 seconds.
Settings for molecular weight, including scale, minimum/maximum values, and units, are available but the molecular weight view is currently disabled.
Dataview obtains settings for viscosity, density, temperature, pressure units, ranges, and time interval from the 'Settings' tab of RCP.
diagram_labels: Select Parameter to Display on each axis
